title: AI Music Generator Suno Suffers Data Leak Affecting 55.3 Million Users and Training Data Source Code
excerpt: According to the data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned, the AI music generator Suno was recently attacked in November 2025, resulting in the leakag
content_html:
According to the data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned, the AI music generator Suno was recently attacked in November 2025, resulting in the leakage of personal information of more than 55.3 million users. The stolen sensitive data includes customer names, actual and email addresses, phone numbers, purchase records, as well as partial credit card numbers and expiration dates extracted from Stripe accounts.

Although this security incident occurred several months ago and was recently exposed by media outlets such as 404Media, Suno had not previously proactively notified affected users or publicly disclosed the details. A company spokesperson, Rachel Racusen, later confirmed the existence of the security incident in November 2025 and did not deny the scale of affected individuals.

More critically, the leaked dataset also included Suno's source code, directly exposing the company's operations of scraping millions of songs and lyrics from well-known platforms such as Deezer, Genius, and YouTube to train its AI model.

Currently, Suno is facing a joint lawsuit from several major record companies, accused of copyright infringement due to large-scale scraping of copyrighted works. This security and data breach incident not only reveals shortcomings in user data protection in consumer-level AI applications, but the disclosed source code may also provide key evidence for legal accountability regarding the compliance and data sources of generative AI training, further increasing regulatory and compliance risks in this field.

sentences.zh: 此次安全和数据泄露事件不仅暴露了消费级人工智能应用中用户数据保护的缺陷，而且被披露的源代码还可能为生成式人工智能训练的合规性和数据来源的法律责任提供关键证据，进一步增加该领域的监管和合规风险。

content_html_zh:
据数据泄露通知服务 Have I Been Pwned 称，AI 音乐生成器 Suno 最近于 2025 年 11 月遭到攻击，导致超过 5530 万用户的个人信息泄露。 被盗的敏感数据包括客户姓名、实际地址和电子邮件地址、电话号码、购买记录，以及从 Stripe 账户中提取的部分信用卡号码和到期日期。

尽管这起安全事件发生在几个月前，并于近期被404Media等媒体曝光，但Suno此前并未主动通知受影响的用户，也没有公开披露细节。 公司发言人 Rachel Racusen 随后确认了 2025 年 11 月发生的安全事件的存在，并没有否认受影响个人的规模。

更关键的是，泄露的数据集还包括Suno的源代码，直接暴露了该公司从Deezer、Genius、YouTube等知名平台抓取数百万首歌曲和歌词来训练其AI模型的操作。

目前，Suno正面临几大唱片公司的联合诉讼，被指控因大规模抄袭版权作品而侵犯版权。 此次安全和数据泄露事件不仅暴露了消费级人工智能应用中用户数据保护的缺陷，而且被披露的源代码还可能为生成式人工智能训练的合规性和数据来源的法律责任提供关键证据，进一步增加该领域的监管和合规风险。
